name: developer-roadmap
description: Interactive roadmaps, guides and other educational content to help developers grow in their careers.
what_they_build: Interactive developer career roadmaps for frontend, backend, DevOps, React, Vue, Angular, etc. — one of the most-starred educational repos on GitHub.
contribution_needs: Open issues available; content updates, new roadmaps, translations, and diagram improvements always welcome.

name: system-design-primer
description: Learn how to design large-scale systems. Prep for the system design interview. Includes Anki flashcards.
what_they_build: Comprehensive system design study guide with Anki decks, covering load balancing, caching, databases, microservices, and real-world case studies.
contribution_needs: 527 open issues; contributions welcome for new content, corrections, translations, and additional system design examples.

name: SponsorBlock
description: Skip YouTube video sponsors — browser extension that community-moderates sponsor segments.
what_they_build: Crowdsourced browser extension that skips sponsored segments in YouTube videos using a community-maintained database. Powers sponsor detection for millions of users.
contribution_needs: 413 open issues; needs help with category improvements, API work, machine learning on sponsor detection, and browser extension development.

name: kana-dojo
description: Aesthetic, minimalist platform for learning Japanese inspired by Duolingo and Monkeytype. Beginner-friendly with plenty of good first issues.
what_they_build: Open-source Japanese learning platform built with Next.js, featuring typing-based practice and gamified learning. Sponsored by Vercel.
contribution_needs: 262 open issues; explicitly beginner-friendly with 'good first issue' labels, first-timers-only contributions welcome.

name: Flask, Jinja, Click, Werkzeug, Quart
description: The Pallets organization develops Flask, Jinja, Click, Quart, and other popular Python libraries that power applications of all sizes worldwide.
what_they_build: The most widely-used Python web framework ecosystem: Flask (71k stars), Jinja templating (11.6k), Click CLI (17.4k), Werkzeug (6.9k), and async Quart.
contribution_needs: Maintained by a small team; accepting contributions for docs, bug fixes, and new features across all packages.

name: vue-router & pinia (Vue.js Official)
description: Eduardo (posva) — Vue.js core team — maintains the official Vue Router and Pinia (store). Also builds vuefire and other Vue ecosystem tools.
what_they_build: Official Vue.js Router (4.6k stars) and Pinia state management (14.6k stars) — core Vue ecosystem libraries. Also vuefire (Firebase bindings) and pinia-colada (data fetching).
contribution_needs: Active maintenance with open issues across repos; TypeScript contributions, bug fixes, and new feature development welcome.

name: Makie.jl
description: Makie is a modern, open-source plotting infrastructure for Julia — interactive data visualizations with GPU acceleration.
what_they_build: High-performance Julia plotting ecosystem with Makie.jl (2.7k stars), AlgebraOfGraphics.jl, GeoMakie.jl for geographic plots, and GraphMakie.jl for network visualization.
contribution_needs: Multiple sub-packages have open issues; GeoMakie has 87 open issues, AlgebraOfGraphics has 61 — GPU backends, new plot types, and documentation always needed.

name: neomacs
description: NEO Emacs: A GPU-powered Emacs written in Rust with a modern display engine. Aiming for 10x performance, 100% Emacs compatibility, 4K video/images via GPU acceleration.
what_they_build: A reimagined Emacs built with Rust and GPU rendering. Modern architecture with multi-threaded Elisp, DMA-BUF zero-copy, rich animation support, and WPE/WebKit integration.
contribution_needs: 28 open issues; actively seeking contributors for Rust backend, Elisp compatibility layer, GPU rendering pipeline, and documentation.

name: pyGAM
description: [CONTRIBUTORS WELCOME] Generalized Additive Models in Python — interpretable machine learning for tabular data.
what_they_build: Python library for Generalized Additive Models (GAMs) — interpretable ML models that balance prediction accuracy with model interpretability for tabular data.
contribution_needs: Explicitly marks '[CONTRIBUTORS WELCOME]' in description; needs help with new GAM model types, performance optimization, documentation, and scikit-learn compatibility.

name: Domain Digger
description: Open-source domain analysis tool focused on outstanding UX, built by Felix Wotschofsky.
what_they_build: Open-source domain analysis tool with a focus on great user experience. For developers and security researchers analyzing domain portfolios.
contribution_needs: Early-stage project; actively seeking contributors for new features, UI/UX improvements, and additional domain analysis capabilities.

name: Open Sheet Music Display
description: TypeScript-based open source library for displaying MusicXML sheet music. Helps developers build digital sheet music applications.
what_they_build: TypeScript library that renders MusicXML sheet music in the browser. Enables developers to build musicXML sheet music display apps.
contribution_needs: Open source project seeking support to continue building state-of-the-art sheet music rendering for digital music applications.

name: AxePress Development (WPGraphQL)
description: Supporting WPGraphQL ecosystem — GraphQL API for WordPress. AxePress Development builds open-source tools around WordPress and GraphQL.
what_they_build: Open-source tools improving the WordPress GraphQL ecosystem — WPGraphQL plugin and related integrations for headless WordPress setups.
contribution_needs: Growing ecosystem with opportunities in WPGraphQL extensions, React/TypeScript frontend tooling, and WordPress plugin development.

name: jub0bs (Go developer)
description: Go developer and trainer, infosec enthusiast. Building and teaching Go with a focus on web security and minimalist design.
what_they_build: Go packages and developer training content — focused on web security, minimalist APIs, and Go best practices.
contribution_needs: Individual developer; open to collaboration on Go packages, security tooling, and educational content for Go developers.

name: OpenAQ Platform
description: OpenAQ is a nonprofit fighting air inequality with open data and open-source tools. Real-time and historical air quality data from around the world.
what_they_build: Open-source platform aggregating real-time and historical air quality data from governments and sensors worldwide. Powers air quality apps and research.
contribution_needs: Nonprofit with open source tools; needs contributors for data ingestion pipelines, API improvements, data visualization, and community sensors.

name: ImageIO
description: The easy (and performant) way to read/write image and video data in Python. Scientific computing image library.
what_they_build: Python library for reading and writing images and videos — widely used in scientific computing and data processing pipelines.
contribution_needs: Active open source project seeking contributors for new format support, performance improvements, and documentation.

name: slop (Ruby CLI)
description: Simple Lightweight Option Parsing — ✨ new contributors welcome ✨. A Ruby gem for command-line option parsing.
what_they_build: Minimalist Ruby gem for parsing command-line options. Clean API, beginner-friendly, suitable as a first open-source contribution.
contribution_needs: Explicitly says '✨ new contributors welcome ✨'; 2 open issues — good first issue for Ruby developers new to open source.

name: publiclab/plots2
description: A collaborative knowledge-exchange platform in Rails; we welcome first-time contributors!
what_they_build: Collaborative knowledge platform built with Ruby on Rails — community-driven research and documentation on environmental topics.
contribution_needs: 581 open issues; explicitly welcomes first-time contributors, strong community mentorship, labeled 'good first issue' and 'help wanted' tickets.

name: QGIS
description: A Free and Open Source Geographic Information System — desktop GIS application used worldwide for mapping and spatial analysis.
what_they_build: One of the world's leading desktop GIS applications — cross-platform mapping, spatial analysis, and geospatial data visualization.
contribution_needs: Large open source project with many areas for contribution: C++ core, Python plugins, documentation, translations, and testing.
